metadata.notes: two agy in one cwd = two independent conv-id transcripts/sessions; daemon keeps them DISTINCT (unique <conv-id> dirs), never merges. Transcript-first: conv-id sessions are pid=0 (no PID binding/disambiguation). Driver start_session/session/wait_turn ported; recorded clean (two distinct session_ids, 7/7 phases).

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready
route: record (driver gap ported)

## Reasoning

**Agent (yes).** Two `agy` CLI processes can run concurrently in the same cwd. `agy` is a standalone native binary with 1 process per conversation, and each conversation gets its own independent top-level transcript dir under `~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/brain/<conv-id>/` — confirmed by multiple sibling conv dirs in the real store. Nothing prevents two from sharing a working directory.

**Daemon (full).** This scenario is the heart of the adapter's transcript-first design and it handles it correctly:

- `sessionIDFromPath` mints exactly one session per `<conv-id>` transcript, so two concurrent agy in the same cwd yield two DISTINCT `session_id`s with independent working→ready arcs derived purely from their own transcripts (`adapter.go`).
- Distinctness is STRUCTURAL and transcript-first: a conv-id session never binds a PID (pid=0 — agy reports its sandbox scratch dir as the run_command cwd and most turns carry no cwd, so the conv-id transcript session has no cwd and no PID). The two rows stay distinct because their `<conv-id>` dirs are unique, not via any PID disambiguation. (A separate proc-<pid> session minted by the `agy` scanner does NOT merge with a conv-id session — record confirmed the conv-id sessions alone carry the lifecycle arcs.)
- State transitions are per-session (parser is per-conversation), so a turn in one session never moves the other — exactly the no-cross-contamination assertion.

The scenario's assertions (two distinct session_ids + PIDs, independent arcs, stable binding) all map to events the adapter produces. Crucially, this scenario asserts NO token/cost metrics — only lifecycle + distinctness + binding — so the fact that Antigravity omits usage from the JSONL does not weaken the verdict.

**Driver (gap:start_session).** The recipe needs three step types the scaffolded `driver-interactive.sh` does NOT yet elicit: `start_session` (launch the 2nd concurrent agy), `session` (route later steps back to session 1), and `wait_turn` (the turn-done poll). The driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS` is only `"send slash sleep"`; `wait_turn`, `start_session`, and `session` are present as arms but stubbed (`not_implemented`), and `wait_turn` (SEAM 2) is unimplemented. These were ported from the codex slot-model reference driver (commit teaching antigravity the start_session + session step types) and recorded clean. NOT an observability limit.

## What record must do

Port SEAM 2 (`wait_turn` — poll the transcript for a terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls, the parser's turn_done signal) and the slot-model `start_session`/`session` arms (alloc_slot/load_slot), then drive the two-session recipe.
caveats: daemon=full is derived from reading the adapter (transcript-first one-session-per-conv + cwd PID disambiguation); it is provisional until record proves two concurrent agy in one cwd produce two distinct, independent, non-flapping session rows.
wait_turn (SEAM 2) is unimplemented in the scaffolded driver — the same gap affects every script recipe for this column; record ports it once.

RECORD CORRECTION (2026-06-20): the assessment's 'highest-unclaimed-PID disambiguation' premise is WRONG for antigravity. The live recording (conv-ids a42452a7 + fffe1aa0 in one cwd) showed two distinct transcript-first sessions, each pid=0 with arcs derived purely from its own <conv-id> transcript ('new session created' / 'force ready->working on first activity' / 'agent finished turn -> ready' — no pid_discovered/process_exited). Distinctness is structural (unique conv-id dirs), not PID-based. 7/7 phases passed.

sources.note: sessionIDFromPath mints one session per <conv-id> transcript; two concurrent agy in one cwd => two distinct session_ids
DiscoverPID matches agy by name+cwd; highest-unclaimed PID disambiguator keeps the two same-cwd sessions' bindings stable (lowest-PID fallback)
DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash sleep'; wait_turn (SEAM 2), start_session, and session are stubbed (not_implemented) — must be ported before recording
Multiple sibling conv dirs each with its own transcript.jsonl — confirms one independent transcript per conversation

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth_creds.json under ~/.gemini)
irrlichd is recording (isolated mode spawns its own --record daemon, or --attach against a --record daemon)
no other agy session is live in the run cwd
setup: The start_session step launches session 2 (slot 2) in the SAME cwd as a separate agy process (separate conversation/transcript)
Steps carrying {"session":1} route send/wait_turn back to session 1 while session 2 stays alive

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: alpha
Reply with exactly the word: bravo
Reply with exactly the word: alpha2

verify: events.jsonl contains 2 distinct antigravity session_ids (two <conv-id> dirs), both keyed to the same cwd slug
each session_id has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other
session 1 shows TWO working->ready arcs (turn1 + the post-start_session re-drive); session 2 shows ONE
no state flapping: the daemon never swaps the PID-to-session binding between the two rows
